title: Feb 11, 2025 10:16AM
content:
Feb 11, 2025 10:16AM
Raniyah Lockley
You made some great points about Phoenix’s name and the path showing her strength and sacrifice. Another symbol that stood out to me was the obstacles she faces along the way, like the thorns and the barbed wire fence. These aren’t just physical struggles they represent all the hardships she’s had to push through in life.

For example, when she gets caught on a bush, “She kept stretching her hand up, but she could not reach. A bush caught her dress.” (Welty, 1941). This moment feels like a metaphor for how life keeps trying to hold her back, but she refuses to stop. It’s a small detail, but it reinforces how determined she is.

The scarecrow scene is another moment that shows her resilience. At first, she’s scared, thinking it’s a ghost, but when she realizes what it is, she laughs: “My senses is gone. I too old. I the oldest people I ever know.” (Welty, 1941). Even when she’s tired and struggling, she still finds humor in the situation. That says a lot about her mindset she’s not just enduring hardships, she’s pushing through them with grace.
